title: podman image scp ignoring SSH key configured in system connection
body:
### Issue Description

I try to scp an image to an ARM SBC (Orange Pi Zero) running Armbian.

Command:
```shell
podman image scp myimage:latest orangepizero::
```

Podman appears to ignore the ed25519 SSH key given in the system connection and uses ssh-agent instead.

I am confused because I can add the connection without an issue and am using the key to connect to the host via SSH.

podman system connection list
```log
Name          URI                                                              Identity                         Default
orangepizero  ssh://florian@orangepizero:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  /home/florian/.ssh/orangepizero  true
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create ed25519 ssh-key
2. Add connection with `podman system connection add --identity ~/.ssh/keyname CONNECTION user@host`
3. Try to scp an image with `podman image scp myimage:latest CONNECTION::`


### Describe the results you received

`podman image scp` appaears to ignore the SSH key given in the system connection and uses ssh-agent instead, which in turn tries every key it knows, leading to the following error:
```log
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: disconnect, reason 2: Too many authentication failures
```
Sometimes I also get the following error:
```log
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: agent: failed to sign challenge
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman uses the SSH key given in the system connection and `podman image scp command` is successful.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Target is an ARM SBC (Orange Pi Zero) running Ubuntu Jammy based Armbian.

sshd version: `OpenSSH_8.9p1 Ubuntu-3ubuntu0.6, OpenSSL 3.0.2 15 Mar 2022`

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

also seeing this issue on debian bookworm release, trying to ``scp`` between two containers which already have ``.ssh/authorized_keys`` entries registered both ways between both containers.
podman doesn't seem to try the ``pubkey`` auth method but still asks for the password of the target machine of the copy instruction.

Similar issue here, server requires a pubkey, ssh works fine.
```
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none], no supported methods remain
```
